title: Aeranthes elegans
taxonomy: Aeranthes elegans is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This genus is further classified as a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Vandeae. Aeranthes elegans is native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. It is an epiphytic plant that grows in the wild, attaching itself to other plants for support.

intro: Aeranthes elegans, also known as the "orchid of the forest," is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae) native to Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Aeranthes elegans contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal effects.
